LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE OCT-13-2020 HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS GORDON SCOTT, CLAY GIBSON, MARK FRATKIN, PAUL MIGDAL, STEVE SEVERAID, GREENSPAN ADJUSTERS INTERNATIONAL, INC., ADJUSTERS INTERNATIONAL PACIFIC NORTHWEST, INC., AND THE GREENSPAN COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS, THE COURT ORDERS AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS IS TO BE CONTINUED TO A DATE TO BE DETERMINED. THE PAPERS RAISE A FACTUAL DISPUTE ABOUT WHETHER PLAINTIFF SIGNED THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT. THE COURT MUST RESOLVE THE FACTUAL DISPUTE TO DETERMINE WHETHER A VALID ARBITRATION AGREEMENT EXISTS. (ROSENTHAL, TOAL V. TARDIF (2009) 178 CAL.APP.4TH 1208, 1219-1220 ["BEFORE GRANTING A PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION, [A COURT] MUST DETERMINE THE FACTUAL ISSUE OF 'THE EXISTENCE OR VALIDITY OF THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT.'"], QUOTING ROSENTHAL V. GREAT WESTERN FIN. SECURITIES CORP. (1996) 14 CAL.4TH 394, 413.) THE COURT WILL SET AN EVIDENTIARY HEARING ON A DATE TO BE DETERMINED TO TAKE PLACE VIA ZOOM. THE COURT SHALL LIMIT THE EVIDENTIARY HEARING TO THREE HOURS WITH EQUAL TIME ALLOTTED TO EACH SIDE. DIRECT TESTIMONY OF ANY DECLARANT WILL NOT BE PERMITTED, AND ONLY CROSS-EXAMINATION OF DECLARANTS WILL BE ALLOWED. COUNSEL ARE TO MEET AND CONFER ON DISCOVERY AND ON AN AGREED-UPON HEARING DATE FOR THE EVIDENTIARY HEARING WHICH IS TO TAKE PLACE AT 1:30 P.M. PREFERABLY ON EITHER A THURSDAY OR FRIDAY. COUNSEL ARE TO UPDATE THE COURT WITHIN A WEEK BY EMAIL SENT TO CALENDAR302@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
Defendant Greenspan Adjusters International, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Select Allegations Of Plaintiff'S Original And First Amended Complaints
Off Calendar Nov-24-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. YOSEF PERETZ, Esq. of Peretz & Associates (Tel: 415-637-7576) for the plaintiff;
Atty. FRANK CIALONE, Esq. of Shartsis Friese LLP (Tel: 415-773-7298) for defendant Greenspan Adjusters International, Inc.
Atty. MARCIE FITZSIMMONS, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani LLP (Tel: 415-986-5900) appearing for defendants Greenspan Adjusters International, Inc., Adjusters International Pacific Northwest, Inc., The Greenspan Company, Gordon Scott, Clay Gibson, Mark Fratkin, Paul Migdal, and Steve Severaid.

Hearing Re: Defendant Greenspan Adjusters International, Inc.'s Motion To Seal Select Allegations Of Plaintiff's Original And First Amended Complaints.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Greenspan Adjusters International, Inc.'s motion to seal select allegations of Plaintiff's original and first amended complaints is denied. Greenspan fails to show that the requirements of California Rule of Court 2.550 are met, specifically that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; that the overriding interest supports sealing the record; that a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; that the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and that no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. The Fratkin declaration shows only that Plaintiff advised Greenspan on legal matters including "issues related to allegations in the First Amended Complaint"; that general assertion and the single conclusory sentence in the Migdal declaration (par. 20) are entirely inadequate to meet Greenspan's burden under Rule 2.550. Neither the complaint nor the first amended complaint refers on its face to confidential communications protected by the attorney-client privilege. Due to the important constitutional principles at stake in open proceedings in all civil cases, the proponent of a sealing order must make a "specific showing of serious injury" that it would suffer if the documents (or portions of documents) are made available to the public. (Universal City Studios, Inc. v. Superior Court (2003) 100 Cal.App.4th 1273, 1282). "In delineating the injury to be prevented, specificity is essential. Broad allegations of harm, bereft of specific examples or articulated reasoning, are insufficient." (Id., quoting In re Cendent Corp. (3rd Cir. 2001) 260 F.3d 183, 194; see also McNair v. National Collegiate Athletic Assn. (2015) 234 Cal.App.4th 24, 29 [moving party failed to carry its burden to demonstrate that its interest in the confidentiality of enforcement proceedings overrides the constitutional right of access and the presumption of openness of substantive court proceedings in ordinary cases]; Huffy Corp. v. Superior Court (2003) 112 Cal.App.4th 97 [all exhibits in insurance coverage dispute ordered unsealed].)

The Court also notes that although the original complaint was filed on November 27, 2019, Greenspan did not seek to seal the complaint (which to this stay, nearly one year later, remains posted on the Court's register of actions in its original, unredacted form) until months after it removed the case to federal court on February 13, 2020, nor did it seek such prompt relief after the case was remanded on July 6, 2020. Greenspan's lengthy delay in seeking to stay the complaint and first amended complaint strongly suggests that its motivation in seeking to seal the pleadings is to avoid adverse publicity, not to protect genuinely privileged or proprietary information.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Carlito Mandia, CSR No. 9689, cmandia@sftc.org (302/EPS)

Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Oct-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. YOSEF PERETZ, Esq. of PERETZ & ASSOCIATES (Tel: 415-534-6240) for plaintiff;
Atty. MARCIE FITZSIMMONS, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (Tel: 415-986-5900) for Defendants Greenspan Adjusters International, Inc., Adjusters International Pacific Northwest, Inc., The Greenspan Company, Gordon Scott, Clay Gibson, Mark Fratkin, Paul Migdal, and Steve Severaid;
Atty. FRANK CIALONE, Esq. of Shartsis Friese LLP (Tel: 415-421-6500) for Defendant Greenspan Adjusters International, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS GORDON SCOTT, CLAY GIBSON, MARK FRATKIN, PAUL MIGDAL, STEVE SEVERAID, GREENSPAN ADJUSTERS INTERNATIONAL, INC., ADJUSTERS INTERNATIONAL PACIFIC NORTHWEST, INC., AND THE GREENSPAN COMPANY's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

Natter argued. The Court orders as follows: Defendants' Motion to Compel Arbitration and Stay Proceedings is to be continued to a date to be determined. The papers raise a factual dispute about whether Plaintiff signed the arbitration agreement. The Court must resolve the factual dispute to determine whether a valid arbitration agreement exists. (Rosenthal, Toal v. Tardif (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 1208, 1219-1220 ["before granting a petition to compel arbitration, [a court] must determine the factual issue of 'the existence or validity of the arbitration agreement.'"], quoting Rosenthal v. Great Western Fin. Securities Corp. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 394, 413.) The Court will set an evidentiary hearing on a date to be determined to take place via Zoom. The Court shall limit the evidentiary hearing to three hours with equal time allotted to each side. Direct testimony of any declarant will not be permitted, and only cross-examination of declarants will be allowed. Counsel are to meet and confer on discovery and on an agreed-upon hearing date for the evidentiary hearing which is to take place at 1:30 p.m. preferably on either a Thursday or Friday. Counsel are to update the court within a week by email sent to Calendar302@sftc.org.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order To Stay Discovery
Off Calendar Oct-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Yosef Peretz and David Garibaldi for Plaintiff.
Marcie Fitzsimmons and Frank Cialone for Defendants.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTs GORDON SCOTT, CLAY GIBSON, MARK FRATKIN, PAUL MIGDAL, STEVE SEVERAID, GREENSPAN ADJUSTERS INTERNATIONAL, INC., ADJUSTERS INTERNATIONAL PACIFIC NORTHWEST, INC., THE GREENSPAN COMPANY's Motion For Protective Order To Stay Discovery

The Motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: Plaintiff shall be entitled to propound discovery relating solely to questions of arbitrability until such time as the court rules on defendants' motion to compel arbitration. If the motion to compel arbitration is denied, all parties are free to propound appropriate discovery including such discovery as has already been served. Discovery open for 14 days on arbitration issue.

Counsels to meet and confer with respect to limit on discovery. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the adopted ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
